contextual.title: Voice and Swallowing Outcomes Following Revision Anterior Cervical Spine Surgery
contextual.text:
Summary: Evaluate voice and swallowing outcomes post operatively.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 Patients undergoing revision anterior cervical spine surgery 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 Primary pathology of the upper aero-digestive tract other than mild chronic pharyngitis, pre-existing vocal cord paralysis.

metadata.brief_title: Voice and Swallowing Outcomes Following Revision Anterior Cervical Spine Surgery
metadata.inclusion_criteria:
inclusion criteria: 

 Patients undergoing revision anterior cervical spine surgery
metadata.exclusion_criteria:
: 

 Primary pathology of the upper aero-digestive tract other than mild chronic pharyngitis, pre-existing vocal cord paralysis.
metadata.brief_summary: Evaluate voice and swallowing outcomes post operatively.

Dysphagia.definition: Difficulty in SWALLOWING which may result from neuromuscular disorder or mechanical obstruction. Dysphagia is classified into two distinct types: oropharyngeal dysphagia due to malfunction of the PHARYNX and UPPER ESOPHAGEAL SPHINCTER; and esophageal dysphagia due to malfunction of the ESOPHAGUS.
